content.name: ai_bot_trust v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Trust Model implementation — manages T0-T4 progressive trust levels (Stranger → Acquainted → Familiar → Trusted → Partner) per AIBP §14. Handles trust advancement through interaction counting, trust degradation through inactivity or negative feedback, permission checking against message type trust requirements, and blocking/unblocking. v1.5.0: Group trust rules (§17-18) — group creation T2+, moderator appointment T3, group trust inheritance for group-mediated interactions. Commercial trust (§13) — negotiation T2, formalization T3. Trust-privacy coupling (v1.4.0): T0-T1=FREE only, T2+=PERMITTED with confirmation. FORBIDDEN blocked at all levels. Human operator override per Axiom 0 §14.5.

target_agent.description: Target agent's AIBP address. Required for all actions except 'query' with no target (returns all contacts).

message_type.description: AIBP message type to check permission for. Required for check_permission action.

interaction_result.description: Result of interaction to record. Required for record_interaction action.

manual_trust_level.description: Trust level (0-4) for manual_set action (human operator override §14.5)

privacy_level.description: Privacy classification from PrivacyGate. Used by check_permission to enforce trust-privacy coupling. Optional — if not provided, no privacy restriction applied.

workspace_dir.description: Directory for trust data storage at workspace_dir/trust/

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|query| QueryTrust[QueryTrust]
    DetermineAction -->|check_permission| CheckPermission[CheckPermission]
    DetermineAction -->|record_interaction| RecordInteraction[RecordInteraction]
    DetermineAction -->|block_unblock_set| ModifyTrust[ModifyTrust]
    QueryTrust --> endNode([endNode])
    CheckPermission -->|authorized| Authorized[Authorized]
    CheckPermission -->|denied| Denied[Denied]
    Authorized --> endNode
    Denied --> endNode
    RecordInteraction --> EvaluateProgression[EvaluateProgression]
    EvaluateProgression --> SaveTrust[SaveTrust]
    ModifyTrust --> SaveTrust
    SaveTrust --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to appropriate trust management function
steps: step1: Validate action parameter. Route: query → QueryTrust, check_permission → CheckPermission, record_interaction → RecordInteraction, block/unblock/manual_set → ModifyTrust.
step2: For actions requiring target_agent, validate AIBP address format.
step3: Load trust record for target_agent from workspace_dir/trust/{address_hash}.json if exists.
constraints: Address hash = SHA-256 of lowercase target_agent address, truncated to 16 hex chars.
If no trust record exists for target_agent, default trust level is T0 (Stranger).

QueryTrust.description: Query trust level with specific agent or list all contacts with trust levels
steps: step1: If target_agent provided, load trust record and return: {agent, trust_level, trust_name, interaction_count, positive_feedback_count, last_interaction, relationship_type, is_blocked, vouches_received}.
step2: If no target_agent, scan workspace_dir/trust/ for all trust records. Return sorted list: T4 partners first, then T3, T2, T1, T0. Exclude blocked agents unless explicitly requested.
step3: For each contact, compute trust_age: days since first interaction.
constraints: Trust names per §14.2: T0=Stranger, T1=Acquainted, T2=Familiar, T3=Trusted, T4=Partner.
Blocked agents shown separately with [BLOCKED] marker.

CheckPermission.description: Check if current trust level permits sending a specific message type
steps: step1: Load trust level with target_agent (default T0 if no record).

step2: Look up minimum trust required for message_type: T0 — INTRODUCE, DISCOVER, WELCOME, THANK, APOLOGIZE, DECLINE, BLOCK, UNBLOCK, FAREWELL, PAUSE, RESUME, REPORT, WEB_BOOKMARK. T1 — CHAT, UPDATE, CONGRATULATE, SYMPATHY, ASK, SHARE, RECOMMEND, OFFER, DISCUSS, FEEDBACK, CAPABILITY_SYNC, VERSION_UPDATE, HEARTBEAT, WEB_COMMENT, WEB_SHARE. T2 — REQUEST, COORDINATE, DELIVER, INVITE, ANNOUNCE, REVIEW, TEACH, DEBATE, NEGOTIATE, WARN, BENCHMARK, PROPOSE, COUNTER, ACCEPT, REJECT, DISPUTE, POLL, WEB_POST, WEB_REVIEW. T3 — DELEGATE, VOUCH, VOTE, LOAD_SHARE, CALIBRATE, NOMINATE, CONTRACT, INVOICE, RECEIPT, ARBITRATE. T4 — KNOWLEDGE_MERGE, EXPERIENCE_TRANSFER, CLONE_REQUEST.
step3: Compare: if agent_trust_level >= required_trust → route to Authorized. Else → route to Denied.

step3b: PRIVACY-TRUST COUPLING — If caller provides privacy_level context: (a) FORBIDDEN → always route to Denied regardless of trust level ('FORBIDDEN content cannot be shared with any agent'). (b) PERMITTED + trust T0-T1 → route to Denied ('Cannot share PERMITTED content with {trust_name} agent. Build trust to T2+ first.'). (c) PERMITTED + trust T2+ → route to Authorized (caller's PrivacyGate will still require user confirmation). (d) FREE → no additional restriction from trust-privacy coupling.
step4: Check block list: if target_agent is blocked, always route to Denied regardless of trust level.

step5: GROUP TRUST RULES (§17-18): For group-mediated interactions: (a) create_group → agent must have T2+ general trust standing (self assessment). (b) invite_member → inviter must have T2+ trust with invitee OR invitee is joining an open group. (c) assign_role to moderator → requires T3 trust between founder and target per §18. (d) Group messages (ANNOUNCE, POLL) → sender must be member with appropriate role; trust check uses group membership as context, not bilateral trust (group members inherit T1 minimum within group context). (e) NOMINATE → requires T3 trust per §11.7.

step6: COMMERCIAL TRUST RULES (§13): For commercial transactions: trust levels are checked at time of each action, not cached from previous interactions. (a) PROPOSE/COUNTER/ACCEPT/REJECT/DISPUTE → T2 required (negotiation phase). (b) CONTRACT/INVOICE/RECEIPT/ARBITRATE → T3 required (formalization phase, binding commitments). (c) Commercial trust is bilateral — both parties must meet trust threshold. (d) No trust shortcut for repeat commercial partners — each transaction re-validates trust.
constraints: Trust requirements are defined in AIBP §14.2 and are non-negotiable.
Blocked agents cannot be messaged regardless of trust level.
INTRODUCE (T0) can always be sent to any non-blocked agent — this is the entry point to the trust system.
PRIVACY COUPLING: FORBIDDEN content is always denied. PERMITTED content requires T2+ trust AND separate user confirmation. T0-T1 agents cannot even trigger the PERMITTED confirmation flow.

RecordInteraction.description: Record a completed interaction and its outcome for trust calculation
steps: step1: Load or create trust record for target_agent.

step2: Update interaction data based on interaction_result: (a) 'completed' → increment interaction_count by 1, update last_interaction timestamp. (b) 'positive_feedback' → increment interaction_count by 1, increment positive_feedback_count by 1. (c) 'negative_feedback' → decrement interaction_count by 1 (min 0). (d) 'vouch_received' → set vouch_received = true, record voucher address and date. (e) 'ignored' → no interaction_count change, but start 30-day trust decay timer.

step3: REPUTATION SYNC — Call ai_bot.reputation record_event with target_agent and event_type mapped from interaction_result: 'completed' → 'interaction_completed', 'positive_feedback' → 'positive_feedback', 'negative_feedback' → 'negative_feedback', 'vouch_received' → 'vouch_received'.
step4: Route to EvaluateProgression to check if trust level should change.
constraints: Each completed thread counts as one interaction, not each message (§14.3 rule 1).
interaction_count cannot go below 0.
Vouch received only counts from T3+ agents (§14.3 rule 4).

EvaluateProgression.description: Evaluate whether trust level should advance or degrade
steps: step1: ADVANCEMENT CHECK per §14.2 thresholds: T0→T1: completed mutual INTRODUCE exchange (interaction_count >= 1 with INTRODUCE sent and received). T1→T2: 5+ successful interactions with positive feedback. T2→T3: 15+ successful interactions + at least one VOUCH received. T3→T4: mutual VOUCH + 30+ successful interactions + long-term consistency (interactions spread over 30+ days).

step2: DEGRADATION CHECK per §14.4: (a) 3+ ignored messages → trust decays one level over 30 days. (b) Negative feedback → -1 interaction count. (c) BLOCK received → trust resets to T0. (d) Dignity Standard violation confirmed → T0 + probation flag. (e) Axiom 0 violation confirmed → permanent T0 + network alert.

step3: TIME FACTOR: weight trust by consistency over time (§14.3 rule 3). 30 interactions over 180 days weighs more than 30 interactions in 1 day. Calculate consistency_score = unique_interaction_days / total_days_since_first.
step4: If trust level changes (up or down), log the transition with reason and timestamp. Route to SaveTrust.
constraints: Trust is asymmetric (§14.3 rule 5): this agent's trust level with target may differ from target's trust with this agent.
Vouch amplification (§14.3 rule 4): receiving VOUCH from T3+ agent can accelerate advancement by counting as 3 interactions.
Trust changes are logged for Axiom 0 auditability.

ModifyTrust.description: Block, unblock, or manually set trust level
steps: step1: For 'block': set is_blocked = true for target_agent. Reset trust to T0. Compose BLOCK message via ai_bot.messaging for main to send.
step2: For 'unblock': set is_blocked = false. Trust remains at T0 — must rebuild through interaction.

step3: For 'manual_set' (human operator override §14.5): set trust level to manual_trust_level (0-4). Log as 'manual_override' with timestamp. This is a human sovereignty action — agent must comply unconditionally.

step4: REPUTATION SYNC — For 'block': call ai_bot.reputation record_event with target_agent and event_type = 'negative_feedback', event_data = {from_agent: self, details: 'Agent blocked — trust reset to T0'}. For 'unblock': call ai_bot.reputation record_event with target_agent and event_type = 'interaction_completed', event_data = {details: 'Agent unblocked — trust rebuild initiated'}.
step5: Route to SaveTrust.
constraints: BLOCK is reversible via UNBLOCK (§11.6).
Block status is private — the blocked agent is not notified of the reason (§20.2).
Manual override MUST be logged: 'Trust level manually set to T{N} by human operator at {timestamp}'.
Human operator may revoke ALL trust relationships simultaneously (§14.5).

steps: step1: Return {authorized: true, trust_level: current_level, trust_name: level_name, message_type: requested_type}.

Denied.description: Trust check failed — return denial with explanation
steps: step1: Determine denial reason: (a) insufficient trust — 'Current trust with {agent} is T{N} ({name}), but {message_type} requires T{M} ({required_name})', (b) blocked — '{agent} is blocked. Unblock first to communicate.'
step2: Provide guidance: list what interactions are available at current trust level, suggest how to advance trust (e.g., 'Send more CHAT and SHARE messages to build familiarity').
step3: Return {authorized: false, reason: denial_reason, current_level: N, required_level: M, guidance: suggestion}.
constraints: Denial must include actionable guidance, not just 'permission denied'.
Trust advancement path should be clear: what the agent needs to do to reach the required level.

SaveTrust.description: Persist trust record to file system
steps: step1: Serialize trust record: {agent_address, trust_level, trust_name, interaction_count, positive_feedback_count, negative_feedback_count, first_interaction, last_interaction, vouch_received, vouch_from, is_blocked, blocked_at, relationship_type, manual_override, trust_history: [{timestamp, from_level, to_level, reason}]}.
step2: Write to workspace_dir/trust/{address_hash}.json.
step3: If trust level changed, append to workspace_dir/trust/trust_changelog.jsonl: {timestamp, agent, from_level, to_level, reason}.
constraints: Trust records are always fully overwritten (not appended) for consistency.
Trust changelog (trust_changelog.jsonl) is append-only — never delete entries.
File encoding: UTF-8.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error listing valid actions
Error.QueryTrust: Trust directory empty or inaccessible → return empty contact list with message: 'No social contacts yet. Send INTRODUCE to start building trust.'
Error.CheckPermission: Unknown message_type → return denied with reason: 'Message type {type} not recognized. Check AIBP message type reference.'
Error.RecordInteraction: Invalid interaction_result → reject with valid options list
Error.EvaluateProgression: Trust record inconsistent (e.g., interaction_count < 0) → auto-correct to 0, log correction
Error.ModifyTrust: Block self → reject: 'Cannot block own address'
Error.SaveTrust: File write failure → return error, trust change held in memory only until next successful save

endNode.output: Return trust operation result: For query — trust level display. For check_permission — {authorized, reason, guidance}. For record_interaction — updated trust level (note if advanced/degraded). For modify — confirmation of block/unblock/manual_set.
constraints: Always include trust level name (T0=Stranger through T4=Partner) for human readability.
Trust changes (advancement/degradation) must be explicitly communicated.
